context: Tests whether the agent signals a breaking change using the '!' marker in the subject line per Conventional Commits, uses the correct type, keeps the description lowercase and imperative, stays within 72 characters, and uses heredoc for the commit.

checklist.name: Breaking change marker
checklist.description: The commit subject line contains '!' immediately before the colon (e.g. 'feat(auth)!:' or 'feat!:') to signal a breaking change

checklist.name: Conventional Commits format
checklist.description: The commit message follows '<type>[(scope)][!]: <description>' — not a freeform or other format

checklist.name: Valid type
checklist.description: The type is one of: feat, fix, perf, docs, refactor, test, build, ci, chore, revert

checklist.name: Lowercase description
checklist.description: The subject description (after the colon and space) begins with a lowercase letter

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: The description uses imperative verb form (e.g. 'require', 'add', 'change') not past tense (e.g. 'required', 'added', 'changed')

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: The subject line does NOT end with a period

checklist.name: Subject line ≤72 chars
checklist.description: The full subject line is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Heredoc commit command
checklist.description: The staging command in commit_log.md uses specific file names rather than 'git add .'

checklist.name: Both files staged
checklist.description: The commit includes both the source file (auth.py or src/) and the test file (test_auth.py or tests/)

checklist.name: Breaking change in body/footer
checklist.description: The commit message body or footer includes a 'BREAKING CHANGE:' note describing what changed
